fund.name: Nippon India Large Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: For steady, 18% of your ₹48k goes here as your anchor. Nippon India Large Cap has been around for 18 years with a rock-solid track record, making it a reliable base that captures the stability of big companies. Even if the market dips by 20%, this fund is likely to recover within a year or two.

fund.name: ICICI Prudential Large & Mid Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: We put a quarter (27%) of your monthly SIP here. This fund has been impressively consistent, delivering around 20% returns over the last 5 years. It blends the safety of large companies with the growth potential of mid-sized ones, helping you beat inflation while keeping risk manageable.

fund.name: BANDHAN Large Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: 22% of your money goes to Bandhan Large Cap to add a different perspective. Despite its smaller size (₹314 Cr AUM), it has shown strong momentum, especially outperforming its benchmark over 5 years. It’s a good complement to Nippon, giving you exposure to India's top companies while keeping fees at just 1%. Over 20 years, the difference in fees really matters.

fund.name: Invesco India Large & Mid Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: We add a smaller chunk (10%) in Invesco India Large & Mid Cap for a second view on mid-cap growth. Over the last 3 years, it delivered 24.7% returns, putting it on par with sector leaders. It’s a smarter bet for capturing growth in emerging companies while keeping the portfolio balanced.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Midcap Fund - Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: You asked for an aggressive stance (11%), so we allocate 5,280 here every month. Motilal Oswal Midcap is a star performer, delivering 23.7% returns over 3 years and 21.7% over 5 years. Mid-caps are where the biggest wealth creation happens—but they can drop 20-30% in a bad year. Still, with a 20-year horizon, you’ll ride through the dips and benefit from compounding.

fund.name: WhiteOak Capital Flexi Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: Flexi-cap funds are the best ‘set and forget’ investments—they automatically shift between large, mid, and small caps based on what’s performing best. At 10% of your SIP, WhiteOak’s flexibility adds a smart layer of diversification. With 20 years ahead, you don’t want to worry about switching your portfolio; the fund manager does it for you.

fund.name: Invesco India Smallcap Fund - Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: We keep these at a healthy 7% (₹3,360/month) for high-growth exposure. Invesco India Smallcap has delivered 25.2% returns over 3 years and 22.2% over 5 years—top-tier numbers. But remember, small caps can drop 30-40% in a bad year. With your 20-year horizon and aggressive risk appetite, this is a smart bet for future wealth creation.

fund.name: Sundaram Long Term Tax Advantage Fund Series I Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: These are ELSS funds—tax-efficient equity funds with a 3-year lock-in. At just 5% (₹2,400/month), they serve as your 80C tax saver while locking in money in growth-oriented companies. Even if you’ve used up your ₹1.5 lakh 80C limit, starting a small ELSS SIP is smart. Your returns will be taxed at 12.5% after long-term gains above ₹1.25 lakh, which is better than slab tax.

plan.setup_phase: 1. Create a direct mutual fund account on Groww, Kuvera, or Coin (use direct plans to save on fees). 2. Start system SIPs: ₹8,640 in Nippon Large Cap, ₹12,960 in ICICI Prudential Large & Mid Cap, ₹10,560 in Bandhan Large Cap, ₹4,800 in Invesco Large & Mid Cap, ₹5,280 in Motilal Oswal Midcap, ₹4,800 in WhiteOak Flexi Cap, ₹3,360 in Invesco Smallcap, and ₹2,400 in Sundaram ELSS. 3. If you have ₹5.6 lakh maturing in FDs, park it in these funds as lumps: ₹1,00,800 in Nippon Large Cap, ₹1,51,200 in ICICI Prudential Large & Mid Cap, ₹1,26,720 in Bandhan Large Cap, ₹57,600 in Invesco Large & Mid Cap, ₹57,600 in Motilal Oswal Midcap, ₹4,800 in WhiteOak Flexi Cap, ₹3,360 in Invesco Smallcap, and ₹2,400 in Sundaram ELSS. 4. Use Razorpay/Nach for auto-SIPs.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Confirm all SIPs are auto-debitting and check your fees. Ensure you’re paying monthly 0.2-0.5% on SIPs (e.g., things likeämfi fees).
After 1 year: Check if Motilal Oswal Midcap has fallen behind its benchmark. If yes, switch to Nippon India Small Cap. For ELSS, check if you’ve used the ₹1.5 lakh tax limit; if not, ramp up ELSS.

After 3 years: If markets have crashed 20-30%, stay invested—20 years is a long time. Rebalance: If a single fund grew more than 20% above its target allocation, move some to others to keep the mix balanced.
Quarterly (every 3 months): Review performance. If Motilal Oswal Midcap or Invesco Smallcap underperforms for two consecutive quarters, exit and reallocate to Invesco Large & Mid Cap.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year (e.g., your birthday), check if your portfolio has drifted. If Midcap/Smallcap grew >20% above target, sell some and buy Large Cap/Flexi Cap. If Flexi Cap fell >15% below target, invest more from maturing FDs. Rebalance by selling the ‘winner’ and buying the ‘loser’ to keep your risk in check—no emotional decisions, just math.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

aps.reasoning: The plan allocates 100% of its equity allocation to actively managed funds (Large Cap, Large & Mid, Mid, Flexi, Small, ELSS). None are index funds, resulting in a low passive fraction. The turnover score is moderate (0.5) due to frequent quarterly reviews and a specific rule to switch Midcaps/Smallcaps if they underperform over two quarters, suggesting active management but not daily trading. Cost emphasis is high (0.8) as the plan repeatedly compares expense ratios, explicitly mentioning 'fees at just 1%,' and emphasizes using direct plans to save on fees. Research score is low (0.2) as the rationale relies on historical returns, AUM, and category labels rather than deep stock-specific fundamental analysis or PEG ratios. Time horizon alignment is very high (0.9) with a 20-year outlook, 'set and forget' language, and emphasis on riding out dips. Portfolio activeness is maximal (0.0) because the portfolio consists entirely of high-conviction active funds (specific managers, segmented large/mid/small caps) without the risk metrics (beta, tracking error) or category diversification that would mimic a passive index.

pqs.reasoning: The plan aligns excellently with the investor's aggressive risk profile, 20-year horizon, and specific goal of wealth creation (medical, inheritance). The equity-heavy mix (>95% equity) is appropriate. Tax efficiency is nearly perfect for a 30% slab investor: ELSS is utilized for 80C + equity benefits, and the mix avoids slab-taxed debt funds. Diversification is moderate-to-poor geographically (100% domestic) and slightly bloated with three separate funds covering similar large/mid cap space (Nippon L/C, ICICI L&M, Bandhan L/C), leading to overlap. Internal consistency is high overall, though minor contradictions exist regarding tax headroom assumptions in the legacy FD deployment advice and narrow switching criteria.
